 {"id":"brenner_bot-v41w","title":"Task: Create Transition History Logging","description":"# Task: Create Transition History Logging

## Objective
Implement persistent logging of all state transitions for audit trail and history queries.

## Implementation Details

### File Location
`apps/web/src/lib/hypothesis-history.ts`

### Storage Structure
```
.research/
└── transitions/
    ├── RS-20251230-transitions.json  # Per-session
    └── ...
```

### File Format
```json
{
  "sessionId": "RS-20251230",
  "transitions": [
    {
      "hypothesisId": "H-RS20251230-001",
      "fromState": "proposed",
      "toState": "active",
      "trigger": "activate",
      "triggeredBy": "user",
      "timestamp": "2025-12-30T10:05:00Z"
    },
    {
      "hypothesisId": "H-RS20251230-001",
      "fromState": "active",
      "toState": "killed",
      "trigger": "kill",
      "triggeredBy": "T-RS20251230-002",
      "reason": "ATAC-seq showed open chromatin, contradicting methylation-only hypothesis",
      "timestamp": "2025-12-31T15:30:00Z"
    }
  ]
}
```

### Core Functions
```typescript
export async function logTransition(transition: StateTransition): Promise<void>

export async function getHypothesisHistory(hypothesisId: string): Promise<StateTransition[]>

export async function getSessionTransitions(sessionId: string): Promise<StateTransition[]>

export async function getTransitionsInRange(
  start: Date,
  end: Date
): Promise<StateTransition[]>

export interface HypothesisTimeline {
  hypothesisId: string;
  statement: string;
  transitions: Array<{
    fromState: HypothesisState;
    toState: HypothesisState;
    trigger: TransitionTrigger;
    timestamp: string;
    triggeredBy?: string;
  }>;
  currentState: HypothesisState;
}

export async function buildHypothesisTimeline(
  hypothesisId: string
): Promise<HypothesisTimeline>
```

### Query Examples
```typescript
// "What killed hypothesis H1?"
const history = await getHypothesisHistory('H-RS20251230-001');
const killTransition = history.find(t => t.toState === 'killed');
// → T-RS20251230-002 killed it at 2025-12-31T15:30:00Z

// "What happened in session RS-20251230?"
const transitions = await getSessionTransitions('RS-20251230');
// → [activate H1, activate H2, kill H1, ...]
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All transitions logged to per-session files
- [ ] Query by hypothesis ID
- [ ] Query by session ID
- [ ] Query by time range
- [ ] Timeline reconstruction shows full history
- [ ] Append-only log (no editing history)

## Dependencies
- Task: Define State Transition Types

## Effort Estimate
~150 lines of code, ~2 hours","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:45:05.370418529-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-31T12:04:03.696867615-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-31T12:04:03.696867615-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented in apps/web/src/lib/schemas/hypothesis-lifecycle.ts: transitionHypothesis function (line 292), TransitionHistoryStore class (line 469), and 72 passing unit tests.  {"id":"brenner_bot-vmsb","title":"EPIC: Testing Infrastructure & Full Coverage (No Mocks)","description":"# Goal
Achieve comprehensive test coverage using **real implementations** where possible.

# Mocking Philosophy (CLARIFIED)

**✅ DO test with real implementations:**
- Internal modules (parsers, state management, utilities)
- DOM rendering via happy-dom
- React hooks and components
- Data transformations and business logic

**✅ ACCEPTABLE to mock/stub:**
- `framer-motion` - Animation timing causes test flakiness
- External network calls in **unit tests** (not E2E) - for isolation
- Browser APIs not available in test env (IntersectionObserver, etc.)

**❌ DO NOT mock:**
- Internal module dependencies (import real modules)
- Component children (render real children)
- State management (use real stores)

# Current State (2025-12-30)
- **Unit tests**: 41 files, 50% coverage threshold
- **E2E tests**: 5 spec files
- **Logging**: Excellent infrastructure exists

# Critical Gaps
**Unit Tests Missing:**
- 16+ UI components (SpotlightSearch, DistillationViewer, SessionForm, nav, etc.)
- Library modules (corpus.ts, prompt-builder.ts, search/*)

**E2E Gaps:**
- Search workflow (MAIN USER JOURNEY - untested!)
- Session lifecycle, error handling, auth flows

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 80% line coverage (up from 50%)
- [ ] All interactive components tested with real DOM
- [ ] All critical user journeys have E2E coverage
- [ ] CI fails on coverage regression

# Structure
- **Unit Tests**: Real DOM, real state, real parsers
- **E2E Tests**: Real application, real data, detailed logging
- **Infrastructure**: Fixtures, coverage enforcement, accessibility","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-30T17:49:24.662121237-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-01T14:34:47.75397559-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-01T14:34:47.75397559-05:00","close_reason":"All children completed: Test Infrastructure Enhancements (4ep3), Unit Test Coverage (8dy3), E2E Integration Tests (hvi7). Coverage thresholds enforced, real implementations used, CI integration complete.","labels":["testing quality"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-vmsb","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T22:35:55.792252655-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-voa","title":"TanStack Query: Migrate Document Viewers to Query Hooks","description":"# Goal
Migrate TranscriptViewer and DistillationViewer to use the new Query hooks instead of receiving data as props from Server Components.

## Background
Currently, document viewers receive pre-fetched data as props:
```typescript
// TranscriptViewer.tsx
interface TranscriptViewerProps {
  data: TranscriptData;  // Passed from Server Component
}
```

This works but means:
1. Data is re-fetched on every navigation
2. No caching between page visits
3. Server must parse on every request

## Migration Strategy

### Option A: Keep Server Fetch + Hydrate (Recommended)
Server Component prefetches, passes to client via HydrationBoundary.
- Best for SEO (content in initial HTML)
- Best for performance (no client-side loading flash)
- Query still provides caching for subsequent navigations

### Option B: Full Client-Side Fetch
Remove server prefetch, let Query handle everything.
- Simpler code
- Shows loading state on first visit
- Worse SEO

**We choose Option A** because the corpus pages are content-heavy and benefit from SSR.

## Implementation

### 1. Update corpus/[doc]/page.tsx
```typescript
import { HydrationBoundary, QueryClient, dehydrate } from '@tanstack/react-query';

export default async function CorpusDocPage({ params }) {
  const queryClient = new QueryClient();
  
  await queryClient.prefetchQuery({
    queryKey: ['corpus', 'doc', params.doc],
    queryFn: () => readCorpusDoc(params.doc),
  });

  return (
    <HydrationBoundary state={dehydrate(queryClient)}>
      <DocumentViewer docId={params.doc} />
    </HydrationBoundary>
  );
}
```

### 2. Update TranscriptViewer
```typescript
export function TranscriptViewer({ docId }: { docId: string }) {
  const { data, isLoading, error } = useCorpusDoc(docId);
  
  if (isLoading) return <TranscriptSkeleton />;
  if (error) return <ErrorBoundary error={error} />;
  
  // ... rest of component
}
```

### 3. Update DistillationViewer similarly

## Considerations

### Skeleton Components
Create loading skeletons that match the layout:
- TranscriptSkeleton: TOC + content placeholders
- DistillationSkeleton: Header + section placeholders

### Error Boundaries
Create error UI for:
- Document not found
- Parse failure
- Network error

### Backward Compatibility
During migration, support both patterns:
- Accept data as prop (old pattern)
- Use Query if docId provided (new pattern)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] TranscriptViewer uses Query hook
- [ ] DistillationViewer uses Query hook
- [ ] SSR still works (content in initial HTML)
- [ ] Navigation between docs is instant (cached)
- [ ] Loading states are graceful
- [ ] Error states are handled

## Files to Modify
- apps/web/src/app/corpus/[doc]/page.tsx
- apps/web/src/components/transcript/TranscriptViewer.tsx
- apps/web/src/components/distillation/DistillationViewer.tsx

## Files to Create
- apps/web/src/components/transcript/TranscriptSkeleton.tsx
- apps/web/src/components/distillation/DistillationSkeleton.tsx","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:15:54.657062985-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T23:48:57.870114122-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T23:48:57.870114122-05:00","close_reason":"Migrated document viewers to TanStack Query hooks with HydrationBoundary for SSR prefetching and navigation caching. Created DocumentContentClient.tsx client component that uses useCorpusDoc hook.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-voa","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-1r5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:16:17.466921916-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
